questions.question: Which ancient Indian university was known for its teachings in Buddhism and attracted students from across Asia?
questions.explanation: Nalanda University was an ancient center of learning in India, renowned for Buddhist studies and attracting students from various countries.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Non-Cooperation Movement against British rule in 1920, promoting non-violent resistance.

questions.question: The Partition of Bengal in 1905 was reversed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Partition of Bengal was annulled in 1911 due to widespread protests and opposition.

questions.question: Which treaty marked the end of the First War of Indian Independence in 1857?
options: There was no treaty; it was a rebellion
questions.explanation: The 1857 rebellion was a widespread uprising; it was not a formal treaty but was suppressed without a treaty signing.

questions.question: Who was the first President of India?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India, serving from 1950 to 1962.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
questions.explanation: Koshi River is called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent flooding.

questions.question: Which is the largest island in India?
questions.explanation: Andaman Islands are the largest group of islands in India geographically.

questions.question: The Thar Desert is primarily located in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert mainly spans Rajasthan, with parts in Gujarat, Punjab, and Haryana.

questions.question: Which resource is India's largest domestic source of energy?
questions.explanation: Coal is the largest domestic energy resource in India, forming the backbone of power generation.

questions.question: Who appoints the Chief Justice of India?
questions.explanation: The President of India appoints the Chief Justice of India based on the recommendation of the Collegium.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 14 to 32 of the Indian Constitution enumerate the Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha has a term of how many years?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha's term is five years unless dissolved earlier.

questions.question: Which body is considered the guardian of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court is the guardian and interpreter of the Constitution.

questions.question: Which fundamental Right guarantees the Right to Equality?
questions.explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

questions.question: The Union Budget of India is presented in which house of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Budget is presented in the Lok Sabha, the lower house of Parliament.

questions.question: What does GDP stand for?
questions.explanation: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product, representing the total value of goods and services produced.

questions.question: Which five-year plan focused on self-reliance and import substitution?
questions.explanation: The Third Plan (1961-1966) emphasized self-reliance and import substitution strategies.

questions.question: Which institution is responsible for Monetary Policy in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) formulates and implements monetary policy.

questions.question: Which scientist is known as the 'Father of Modern Physics'?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein is regarded as the father of modern physics for his theory of relativity.

questions.question: Which element has the highest electronegativity?
questions.explanation: Fluorine has the highest electronegativity among elements.

questions.question: Which part of the human body is primarily affected by Alzheimer’s disease?
questions.explanation: Alzheimer's disease affects the brain, leading to memory loss and cognitive decline.

questions.question: The Hubble Space Telescope orbits the Earth at an altitude of approximately:
questions.explanation: The Hubble Space Telescope orbits roughly 547 km above Earth.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) was adopted in which year?
questions.explanation: CBD was adopted in 1992 during the Earth Summit in Rio de Janeiro.

questions.question: Which of the following is a critically endangered species in India?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River Dolphin is critically endangered due to habitat loss and pollution.

questions.question: Which protected area is known for its population of tigers in India?
questions.explanation: Jim Corbett National Park was the first National Park in India and is famous for tiger conservation.

questions.question: The Kyoto Protocol is related to which global environmental issue?
questions.explanation: The Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty that commits state parties to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 2022.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi is the current Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Which international organization awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2023?
options: International Committee of the Red Cross
questions.explanation: The World Food Programme received the Nobel Peace Prize in 2023 for its efforts to combat hunger.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar has been serving as the External Affairs Minister of India since 2019.
